title: Coelogyne hampelii
intro: Coelogyne hampelii is a species of orchid, specifically a type of terrestrial orchid belonging to the Coelogyne genus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to exhibit potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne hampelii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Coelogyne hampelii is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. The genus Coelogyne includes more than 200 species of orchids, most of which are found in Asia. Coelogyne hampelii is also classified as being part of the subtribe Coelogyneinae. This species is a terrestrial orchid, typically growing in rocky areas and grasslands.
